actors.name: User/Account
actors.description: An externally owned account interacting with the Superfluid protocol, acting as a sender, receiver, stream creator, pool member, or token holder.
capabilities: Create, update, or delete money streams (flows)
Participate in distributions as a pool member
Deploy Super Apps or custom Super Tokens
Stake in TOGA to become a PIC

actors.name: Super App
actors.description: A smart contract registered with the Superfluid Protocol, designed to react to Superfluid agreement actions (streams, distributions) via callbacks.
capabilities: React to stream creation, updates, and deletion
Execute custom logic in callbacks (e.g., mint NFTs, start new streams)
Be registered with the Superfluid Host

actors.name: Sentinel
actors.description: A node monitoring the Superfluid network, specifically Constant Flow Agreements, to close critical or insolvent streams.
capabilities: Earn rewards (buffer deposits or PIC rewards) for closing streams

actors.name: Pool Admin
actors.description: An account responsible for managing a Superfluid Distribution Pool, including unit allocation for pool members.
capabilities: Can be the distributor of funds to the pool

actors.name: Superfluid DAO Participant
actors.description: A participant in the Superfluid DAO, typically holding SUP tokens, involved in protocol governance.
capabilities: Participate in discussions regarding protocol upgrades and parameters

actors.name: PIC (Patrician in Charge)
actors.description: An account that has staked tokens in the TOGA contract to become the primary entity responsible for liquidating critical streams for a specific Super Token, earning rewards during a priority period.
capabilities: Receive rewards for closing streams during the patrician period
Run sentinel software to automate liquidations
Can be slashed if streams become insolvent

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: An individual or entity building applications or tools on top of or interacting with the Superfluid protocol.
capabilities: Submit tokens for listing on the Superfluid Explorer

actors.name: Automation Service (e.g., Auto-Wrap, Schedulers)
actors.description: Off-chain services that automate specific Superfluid operations like token wrapping, stream scheduling, or vesting.
capabilities: Monitor Super Token balances and auto-wrap underlying tokens
Manage vesting schedules including cliff distributions and stream initiation

actors.name: External Oracle
actors.description: An external data provider, such as Coingecko for token prices or a Time-Weighted Average Price (TWAP) oracle, used by Superfluid-related services or applications.

assets.name: Super Token (Generic)
assets.description: A programmable token standard extending ERC20, enabling real-time financial interactions like streaming and distributions. Can be a Wrapper, Pure, or Native Super Token.

assets.name: Underlying ERC20 Token
assets.description: A standard ERC20 token that can be wrapped to become a Super Token (e.g., DAI wrapped into DAIx).

assets.name: Flow/Stream
assets.description: A continuous, second-by-second transfer of Super Tokens between two accounts, defined by a flow rate.

assets.name: Distribution Pool Units
assets.description: Shares representing a member's proportional claim on distributions from a GDA Pool. Can be ERC20-like if configured.

assets.name: Stream Buffer/Deposit
assets.description: An amount of Super Tokens locked by a stream sender as collateral to ensure stream solvency during critical periods.

assets.name: TOGA Stake
assets.description: Super Tokens staked by a PIC in the TOGA contract as a commitment to perform liquidations and as collateral against insolvent streams.

assets.name: SUP Token
assets.description: The native governance token of the Superfluid protocol, used for DAO participation and voting.

assets.name: User Data
assets.description: Arbitrary byte data that can be attached to Superfluid operations, allowing for custom logic and off-chain integrations.

components.name: Superfluid Host Contract
components.description: The central smart contract of the Superfluid protocol. It manages Super Token interactions, agreement registrations, Super App registrations, and callback executions. It is an UUPS Proxy.

components.name: Super Token Contract
components.description: A smart contract representing a specific Super Token (e.g., DAIx). It implements ISuperToken or ISETH and handles balance calculations, wrapping/unwrapping, and standard token operations. It is an UUPS Proxy.

components.name: Constant Flow Agreement (CFAv1)
components.description: A Superfluid agreement smart contract that enables the creation, modification, and deletion of continuous money streams (flows) between accounts. It is an UUPS Proxy.

components.name: General Distribution Agreement (GDAv1)
components.description: A Superfluid agreement smart contract that enables one-to-many or many-to-many distributions of Super Tokens, either instantly or as continuous streams, via Distribution Pools. It is an UUPS Proxy.

components.name: SuperTokenFactory Contract
components.description: A smart contract used to deploy new Super Token contracts (both Wrapper and Pure types). It is an UUPS Proxy.

components.name: SuperfluidPool Contract
components.description: A smart contract instance representing a specific Distribution Pool, created via the GDAv1. It manages member units and distributes tokens. It is an UUPS Proxy.

components.name: TOGA Contract
components.description: The Transparent Ongoing Auction smart contract where aspiring PICs stake tokens to participate in the liquidation process for a specific Super Token.

components.name: CFAv1Forwarder Contract
components.description: An immutable helper smart contract designed for client-side applications to interact with CFAv1 functionalities more easily.

components.name: GDAv1Forwarder Contract
components.description: An immutable helper smart contract designed for client-side applications to interact with GDAv1 functionalities more easily.

components.name: SuperTokenV1Library
components.description: A Solidity library that provides convenient functions for smart contracts to interact with Super Tokens and Superfluid agreements (CFA, GDA).

components.name: Superfluid Subgraph
components.description: An off-chain indexing service (The Graph) that provides queryable data about Superfluid protocol activities across various networks.

components.name: MacroForwarder Contract
components.description: A trusted forwarder contract that allows users to execute pre-defined batch operations (macros) on the Superfluid protocol in a single transaction.

components.name: Superfluid Governance II Contract
components.description: The smart contract facilitating the Superfluid DAO's governance processes, including proposal submission and voting. It is an UUPS Proxy.

components.name: Instant Distribution Agreement (IDAv1)
components.description: A Superfluid agreement smart contract for mass dispersion of Super Tokens to multiple addresses based on distribution shares or 'units' at a fixed gas cost. It is an UUPS Proxy. (Being deprecated in favor of GDAv1)

components.name: ConstantFlowNFT
components.description: Smart contract for non-fungible tokens (NFTs) that represent Money Streaming flows. It is an UUPS Proxy.

components.name: PoolAdminNFT
components.description: NFT contract representing admin rights over a SuperfluidPool. It is an UUPS Proxy.

components.name: PoolMemberNFT
components.description: NFT contract where tokens represent membership within a pool, granting units to members in that pool. It is an UUPS Proxy.

data_flows.data: Wrap/Unwrap request (amount, underlying token for wrap)
data_flows.description: User converts ERC20 tokens to Super Tokens or vice-versa.

data_flows.data: Create/Update/Delete Flow parameters (token, receiver, flowRate, userData)
data_flows.description: User initiates or modifies a money stream via the Host, which calls the CFA.
Host contract delegates flow management to the CFA contract.

data_flows.data: Create Pool parameters (token, admin, config) or Update Member Units (pool, member, units)
data_flows.description: Pool Admin creates a new distribution pool or updates member shares via the Host, which calls the GDA.

data_flows.data: Distribution operation details (pool creation, unit updates, distribution execution)
data_flows.description: Host contract delegates distribution management to the GDA contract.
Host contract triggers callbacks on a registered Super App when relevant agreement actions occur.
Super App calls back into the Host to perform further Superfluid operations.

data_flows.data: Liquidation transaction (close stream for critical account)
data_flows.description: Sentinel submits a transaction to close a critical stream.
User stakes tokens in TOGA to become a PIC.

data_flows.data: Real-time balance updates due to flow
data_flows.description: CFA instructs Super Token contract to adjust real-time balances based on active flows.

data_flows.data: Real-time balance updates due to distribution
data_flows.description: GDA instructs Super Token contract to adjust real-time balances based on distributions.
User interacts with CFAv1Forwarder from a client application.
CFAv1Forwarder calls the Superfluid Host to execute the CFA operation.
User interacts with GDAv1Forwarder from a client application.
GDAv1Forwarder calls the Superfluid Host to execute the GDA operation.

trust_boundaries.name: User Wallet - Protocol Interface
trust_boundaries.description: Separates the user's private key management and transaction signing from the on-chain protocol logic. Protects user assets from direct unauthorized access by the protocol.

trust_boundaries.name: On-Chain (Protocol) - Off-Chain (Sentinel/Automation/API)
trust_boundaries.description: Separates the deterministic on-chain smart contracts from potentially fallible or malicious off-chain services. Protects protocol integrity from off-chain manipulation.

trust_boundaries.name: Superfluid Host - Super App
trust_boundaries.description: Separates the core protocol (Host) from user-deployed Super Apps. Callbacks and registration involve crossing this boundary. Protects Host from malicious Super App code.

trust_boundaries.name: Superfluid Host - Agreement Contracts (CFA/GDA)
trust_boundaries.description: The Host calls registered agreement contracts. Protects Host from faulty agreement logic (though agreements are part of the core protocol).

trust_boundaries.name: Client Application - Forwarder Contracts
trust_boundaries.description: Users interact with immutable forwarder contracts from their client applications (e.g., web UIs). Protects users from interacting with incorrect or malicious contract addresses if they use the official forwarders.

trust_boundaries.name: Protocol - External Oracle
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the Superfluid protocol and external data providers like price oracles (e.g., Coingecko for Stream Accounting API, TWAP for TCA example). Protects protocol from inaccurate oracle data.

behaviors.name: User creates a money stream
behaviors.description: User initiates a transaction to create a continuous flow of Super Tokens to a receiver. The Host calls the CFA.

behaviors.name: User wraps ERC20 tokens into Super Tokens
behaviors.description: User approves the Super Token contract and calls 'upgrade' to convert their ERC20 tokens into the corresponding Super Token version.

behaviors.name: Pool Admin creates a Distribution Pool
behaviors.description: Pool Admin calls the Host (which calls GDA) to deploy a new SuperfluidPool contract for token distributions.

behaviors.name: Pool Admin updates member units in a pool
behaviors.description: Pool Admin calls 'updateMemberUnits' on the SuperfluidPool contract to change a member's share.

behaviors.name: Sentinel liquidates a critical stream
behaviors.description: Sentinel identifies a stream where the sender's balance is zero and submits a transaction to close it, potentially earning a reward.

behaviors.name: Super App reacts to an incoming stream
behaviors.description: The Superfluid Host invokes a callback function (e.g., 'onFlowCreated') on a registered Super App when a stream is created to it.

behaviors.name: User stakes tokens in TOGA
behaviors.description: User sends Super Tokens to the TOGA contract to become the PIC for that token, or to outbid the current PIC.

behaviors.name: Developer deploys a new Super Token
behaviors.description: Developer calls 'createERC20Wrapper' or 'createPureSuperToken' on the SuperTokenFactory to deploy a new Super Token contract.

behaviors.name: User interacts with protocol via CFAv1Forwarder
behaviors.description: User's client application calls a function on the CFAv1Forwarder (e.g., 'createFlow'), which then calls the Superfluid Host.

behaviors.name: User interacts with protocol via GDAv1Forwarder
behaviors.description: User's client application calls a function on the GDAv1Forwarder (e.g., 'createPool'), which then calls the Superfluid Host.

behaviors.name: DAO Participant votes on a proposal
behaviors.description: A SUP token holder casts a vote on an active governance proposal through the Superfluid Governance II contract.

behaviors.name: Auto-Wrap Service wraps tokens
behaviors.description: The Auto-Wrap service, acting on behalf of a user/DAO, calls 'upgrade' on a Super Token contract when the Super Token balance is low.

behaviors.name: User executes a batch operation via MacroForwarder
behaviors.description: User calls 'runMacro' on the MacroForwarder, providing the address of a User-Defined Macro contract and parameters. The MacroForwarder then executes the batch operations defined by the macro by calling the Superfluid Host.
